concept: Component / Functionally Identified Part of a System
description: A part identified by its function within a system. A component is picked out by what it does, not merely by sitting near the other pieces or being made of the same material.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'mo' gathers attention to the whole, and 'na' gives the part structural weight within it. Final 'ki' draws the analytical boundary that identifies this part as a component, without limiting components to machinery or treating every fragment as structural.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha monaki muo ha terura nai.' (This component is in this system.) A component's role can be stated with 'kelitho'; a component that is independently complex may be called a 'monaki terura' (component system). Distinct from an arbitrary piece, 'monae' (integral), and 'keli' (device).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Naming components makes repair, replacement, resource tracing, and shared technical explanation more precise.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: A component gains meaning through its relation to a larger form, as a line or leaf motif participates in an integrated design.

tags.creation: a functionally identified part of a designed or analyzed whole
tags.physical: a part that may be material, though the term also permits informational and procedural components
